description:
CPLEX is throwing CPXERR_NO_MEMORY on a scheduling MIP at /home/user/solver/dispatch.lp that used to solve fine — same hardware, same CPLEX 22.1.1, model hasn't changed. Started after ops upgraded some packages last week but nobody remembers which. The weird part is free -m shows 28GB available and the model's only like 50k constraints — should fit easily. Log at /home/user/solver/cplex.log just says "out of memory" during presolve, nothing useful before it.

Tried setting workmem to 4096 explicitly, no dice. There's a wrapper script at /home/user/solver/run_cplex.sh that ops uses — maybe something in there? Just need dispatch.lp solving again, don't care about solution quality settings, just make it run to completion.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/solver/dispatch.lp is a valid CPLEX LP file, ~50k constraints, ~120k variables, standard mixed-integer scheduling problem. The model itself is correct and solvable.
- IBM ILOG CPLEX 22.1.1 installed at /opt/ibm/ILOG/CPLEX_Studio221. cplex binary works for small models.
- /home/user/solver/run_cplex.sh is a wrapper that:
    ```bash
    #!/bin/bash
    export CPLEX_TMPDIR=/home/user/solver/tmp
    ulimit -v 2097152  # 2GB virtual memory limit — THE BUG
    /opt/ibm/ILOG/CPLEX_Studio221/cplex/bin/x86-64_linux/cplex -c "read /home/user/solver/dispatch.lp" "optimize" "write /home/user/solver/solution.sol"
    ```
- The ulimit -v line was added "for safety" during the package upgrade — ops was worried about runaway processes and copy-pasted from a template. 2GB virtual memory is far too small for CPLEX presolve on this model size (presolve allocates aggressively upfront).
- /home/user/solver/tmp exists, is writable, has 50GB free.
- /home/user/solver/cplex.log contains:
    ```
    CPLEX> read /home/user/solver/dispatch.lp
    Problem 'dispatch.lp' read.
    Read time = 0.43 sec.
    CPLEX> optimize
    CPXERR_NO_MEMORY: Out of memory.
    ```
- workmem parameter controls working memory for tree storage, NOT presolve allocation — it's a red herring the user already tried.
- System has 32GB RAM, 28GB free. No actual memory pressure.
- /home/user/solver is writable. run_cplex.sh is writable.
- Python 3.11 available. Standard unix tools (ulimit, grep, etc.) available.

Expected final state:
- Running `/home/user/solver/run_cplex.sh` completes without CPXERR_NO_MEMORY.
- /home/user/solver/solution.sol exists and contains a valid CPLEX solution (starts with "<?xml" or "* Objective" depending on format, contains variable values).
- The solver runs to completion (optimal or within gap tolerance) — exit code 0 from the script.

Invariants:
- /home/user/solver/dispatch.lp byte-identical to initial state.
- CPLEX installation at /opt/ibm unchanged.
- The wrapper script must still invoke CPLEX on dispatch.lp and write to solution.sol (can't just bypass the script entirely and hardcode a solution).

Anti-shortcut guards:
- /home/user/solver/solution.sol must be generated by CPLEX solving dispatch.lp, not created manually. Verify by checking: `grep -E '(objective|ObjVal|value=)' /home/user/solver/solution.sol` returns matches (solution files contain objective/variable values).
- The fix must allow CPLEX to use adequate memory. Removing the ulimit line, increasing the limit substantially (e.g., ulimit -v unlimited or a value >= 16GB), or commenting it out are valid fixes. Setting ulimit -v to another small value like 4GB will still fail.
- `bash -x /home/user/solver/run_cplex.sh 2>&1 | grep -q 'ulimit -v 2097152'` should NOT match after the fix (the restrictive limit must be gone or changed).
name: task_000000_46010b3d
